stimulus:
Population of Four Australian Cities (in thousands), 1881–1911 [graph]

The graph displays the population for the following four cities across 1881, 1891, 1901, and 1911:

  • Sydney: 220 (1881), 380 (1891), 480 (1901), 630 (1911)
  • Melbourne: 280 (1881), 490 (1891), 495 (1901), 590 (1911)
  • Adelaide: 95 (1881), 130 (1891), 140 (1901), 190 (1911)
  • Brisbane: 32 (1881), 60 (1891), 120 (1901), 145 (1911)

The graph shows the populations of four large Australian cities at the end of the nineteenth and beginning of the twentieth centuries.
stem: According to the graph, which city had the largest population in 1891?
rationale:
Choice C is correct because the graph shows that in 1891 Melbourne had approximately 490 thousand inhabitants, more than any other city listed.

Choice A is incorrect: Sydney had about 380 thousand in 1891, less than Melbourne. Choice B is incorrect: Brisbane had only about 60 thousand in 1891. Choice D is incorrect: Adelaide had about 130 thousand in 1891.
